title: TLS, Authentication, Virtual Hosts, Authorization, and Secrets · Type-System Trace: Authentication Mechanism
summary: A production client uses amqp with guest credentials, grants configure/write/read wildcards across the default virtual host, logs connection URLs, disables certificate verification, and cannot rotate credentials without downtime. Planned artifact: Add and verify the authentication mechanism increment in messaging identity, transport, and least-privilege matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose generated credentials, x509 EXTERNAL, or OAuth 2 identity with explicit broker, client, token, renewal, and revocation support.
Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Authentication Mechanism · Theory Authentication Mechanism
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1945 in a payment-effect relay after an ambiguous provider timeout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate verified TLS and vhost access while preserving separate configure, write, and read permissions; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose generated credentials, x509 EXTERNAL, or OAuth 2 identity with explicit broker, client, token, renewal, and revocation support.
